evals.prompt: Write a spec for an actor that watches a directory for new CSV files, validates their schema against a known set of expected columns, and moves valid files to an output directory. Invalid files should be moved to a quarantine directory with a rejection report.
evals.expected_output: A single actor spec with Purpose, Messaging Protocol, and Workflow sections. Should include defined terms for directories, concrete file operations, and conditional branching for valid/invalid paths.

assertions.text: Has '# ... Actor Specification' title heading
Has '## Actor Purpose' section
Has '## Messaging Protocol' section with '### Receives' and '### Sends' subsections
Has '## Workflow' section with numbered steps
Messaging protocol uses Scala stdlib types where appropriate, Java types fully qualified when used, no external library types, no unnecessary single-field wrapper types
Under ### Receives and ### Sends, each message is one bullet line `- `Signature` - gloss`; do not put the gloss on the following line
Uses second-person voice ('Your purpose is to...' or 'You...')

Definitions completeness: if any `_Term_` appears in Actor Purpose, Messaging Protocol, or Workflow, then either (a) `## Definitions` after Actor Purpose lists every such term with a gloss, or (b) Actor Purpose ends with an explicit pointer (e.g. Definition source) to a shared `specs/*.md` definitions file
Has conditional branching (valid vs invalid file handling)
Spec is under 100 lines

evals.prompt: I need a spec for an orchestrator that coordinates three workers to process a batch of images — resizing, watermarking, and uploading — with up to 2 retries per image if any worker fails.
evals.expected_output: An orchestrator spec plus three subagent specs. The orchestrator should use Spawn Subagent pattern with markdown links, have bounded retry logic, and delegate each stage to a child actor.

assertions.text: Main spec has '# ... Actor Specification' title heading
Main spec has '## Messaging Protocol' with '### Receives' and '### Sends' subsections
Main spec uses 'Spawn the Subagent [Name](path)' pattern with markdown links for child actors
Has explicit retry bound (up to 2 retries / 3 attempts)
Creates separate subagent spec files for workers (not inline)
Messaging protocol uses Scala stdlib types where appropriate, Java types fully qualified when used, no external library types, no unnecessary single-field wrapper types
Under ### Receives and ### Sends, each message is one bullet line `- `Signature` - gloss`; do not put the gloss on the following line
Uses second-person voice

Definitions completeness: in orchestrator and each worker spec, if any `_Term_` appears in Actor Purpose, Messaging Protocol, or Workflow, then that file either has inline `## Definitions` listing every term or ends Actor Purpose with a pointer to a shared `specs/*.md` definitions file
Each spec is under 100 lines

evals.prompt: Create a spec for an actor that receives a PR diff and uses an LLM to generate a code review, then posts the review as GitHub comments. The actor should first analyze the diff to identify files worth reviewing, then spawn an LLM-based reviewer subagent for the actual review generation, and finally post the comments via the GitHub API.
evals.expected_output: A main actor spec plus an LLM reviewer subagent spec. The main spec should have file filtering logic, spawn the LLM subagent, and handle GitHub API posting. The subagent should describe the LLM invocation.

assertions.text: Main spec has '# ... Actor Specification' title heading
Main spec has '## Messaging Protocol' with '### Receives' and '### Sends' subsections
Main spec uses 'Spawn the Subagent [Name](path)' pattern for LLM reviewer
Identifies the LLM step as delegated to a child actor (not done inline)
Workflow steps that are LLM/agentic (including subagent delegation for review generation) start with '(Agentic Step)'
Creates a separate subagent spec file for the LLM reviewer
Messaging protocol uses Scala stdlib types where appropriate, Java types fully qualified when used, no external library types, no unnecessary single-field wrapper types
Under ### Receives and ### Sends, each message is one bullet line `- `Signature` - gloss`; do not put the gloss on the following line
Uses second-person voice

Definitions completeness: in main orchestrator and LLM reviewer specs, if any `_Term_` appears in Actor Purpose, Messaging Protocol, or Workflow, then that file either has inline `## Definitions` listing every term or ends Actor Purpose with a pointer to a shared `specs/*.md` definitions file
Has conditional branching for API failure handling
Each spec is under 100 lines
